dependencies.description: VXLAN requires routable IP connectivity between all VTEP loopback interfaces via the underlay network (OSPF, IS-IS, or BGP)
dependencies.name: Underlay IP reachability between VTEPs

dependencies.description: VXLAN adds 50-54 bytes overhead; underlay MTU must accommodate full inner frame plus encapsulation headers
dependencies.name: MTU 9216+ on underlay

dependencies.description: The NVE (Network Virtualization Edge) interface must be configured with a source-interface (typically a loopback) that provides the local VTEP IP
dependencies.name: NVE interface with source-interface

dependencies.description: Each VLAN that needs to traverse the overlay must be mapped to a unique 24-bit VNI
dependencies.name: VLAN-to-VNI mapping

dependencies.description: BUM traffic handling requires either a static ingress replication list, BGP EVPN-based ingress replication, or a multicast group for underlay BUM transport
dependencies.name: Ingress replication or multicast group for BUM

failure_modes.description: The local VTEP has no remote peers in its peer table. show nve peers returns an empty list, meaning no VXLAN tunnels are formed and no overlay traffic can flow.

resolutions.action: Verify NVE interface status: 'show nve interface'
Verify NVE interface status: 'show nve interface'
Verify NVE interface status: 'show nve interface'
Confirm source-interface loopback is up with IP: 'show interface loopback <N>'
Confirm source-interface loopback is up with IP: 'show interface loopback <N>'
Confirm source-interface loopback is up with IP: 'show interface loopback <N>'
Check underlay routing for remote VTEP loopbacks: 'show ip route <remote-vtep-ip>'
Check underlay routing for remote VTEP loopbacks: 'show ip route <remote-vtep-ip>'
Check underlay routing for remote VTEP loopbacks: 'show ip route <remote-vtep-ip>'
For EVPN mode, verify BGP L2VPN EVPN session: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn summary'
For EVPN mode, verify BGP L2VPN EVPN session: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn summary'
For EVPN mode, verify BGP L2VPN EVPN session: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn summary'
For multicast BUM, verify multicast group is correct and PIM is operational
For multicast BUM, verify multicast group is correct and PIM is operational
For multicast BUM, verify multicast group is correct and PIM is operational

root_causes.cause: NVE source interface down or misconfigured
Underlay routing not advertising VTEP loopback
Multicast group misconfigured (if using multicast-based BUM)
EVPN BGP session not up (if using BGP EVPN)

symptoms: show nve peers is empty
No VXLAN tunnels formed
No overlay traffic flowing between VTEPs

failure_modes.description: VTEP peers are discovered and VNIs are operational but actual data traffic (ping, application flows) fails to traverse the VXLAN fabric.

resolutions.action: Check underlay MTU on all links (must be 9216+ to accommodate VXLAN 50-54 byte overhead): 'show interface <underlay-intf>' on all hops
Check underlay MTU on all links (must be 9216+ to accommodate VXLAN 50-54 byte overhead): 'show interface <underlay-intf>' on all hops
Check underlay MTU on all links (must be 9216+ to accommodate VXLAN 50-54 byte overhead): 'show interface <underlay-intf>' on all hops
Verify bidirectional underlay reachability between VTEPs: ping from source-interface loopback
Verify bidirectional underlay reachability between VTEPs: ping from source-interface loopback

Verify ARP suppression table entries: 'show ip arp suppression-cache detail'
Verify ARP suppression table entries: 'show ip arp suppression-cache detail'
Verify ARP suppression table entries: 'show ip arp suppression-cache detail'

root_causes.cause: Underlay MTU too small (need 50+ bytes for VXLAN overhead; 9216 jumbo recommended)
Source interface IP not in remote VTEP's routing table
Asymmetric routing issue in VXLAN fabric
ARP suppression issue preventing address resolution

symptoms: Ping fails across VXLAN fabric
Application traffic not reaching remote hosts
VTEP peers shown but traffic black-holed

failure_modes.description: Inter-subnet routed traffic across the VXLAN fabric is silently dropped because the L3VNI SVI is missing the 'ip forward' command. The L3VNI appears operationally up in 'show nve vni' but routed traffic is not forwarded between subnets through the overlay.

resolutions.action: Add 'ip forward' under the L3VNI SVI: 'interface vlan <L3VNI-VLAN>' → 'ip forward'
Add 'ip forward' under the L3VNI SVI: 'interface vlan <L3VNI-VLAN>' → 'ip forward'
Add 'ip forward' under the L3VNI SVI: 'interface vlan <L3VNI-VLAN>' → 'ip forward'

root_causes.cause: 'ip forward' missing on L3VNI SVI (NX-OS requires this explicitly)
SVI for L3VNI not in 'up/up' state
VRF not associated with L3VNI

Intra-subnet (same VNI) traffic works but cross-VNI traffic is dropped
show nve vni shows L3VNI as Up but routed traffic black-holed
show ip route vrf shows routes present but traffic not forwarded

failure_modes.description: ARP suppression is enabled on an L2VNI but the distributed anycast gateway is not configured for that SVI. The VTEP suppresses ARP requests locally but cannot provide proxy-ARP responses because it has no gateway IP/MAC, causing ARP resolution to fail for hosts behind remote VTEPs.

resolutions.action: Configure anycast gateway MAC globally: 'fabric forwarding anycast-gateway-mac <mac>'
Configure anycast gateway MAC globally: 'fabric forwarding anycast-gateway-mac <mac>'
Configure anycast gateway MAC globally: 'fabric forwarding anycast-gateway-mac <mac>'
Enable on SVI: 'interface vlan <X>' → 'fabric forwarding mode anycast-gateway'
Enable on SVI: 'interface vlan <X>' → 'fabric forwarding mode anycast-gateway'
Enable on SVI: 'interface vlan <X>' → 'fabric forwarding mode anycast-gateway'
Alternatively, disable ARP suppression if anycast gateway is not desired: under NVE member VNI, remove 'suppress-arp'
Alternatively, disable ARP suppression if anycast gateway is not desired: under NVE member VNI, remove 'suppress-arp'
Alternatively, disable ARP suppression if anycast gateway is not desired: under NVE member VNI, remove 'suppress-arp'

root_causes.cause: ARP suppression enabled without distributed anycast gateway on SVI
Anycast gateway MAC not configured globally ('fabric forwarding anycast-gateway-mac')
SVI missing 'fabric forwarding mode anycast-gateway'

failure_modes.scenario: ARP suppression without anycast gateway causing silent drops
symptoms: ARP resolution fails for remote hosts in EVPN fabric
Local ARP requests suppressed but no reply generated
show ip arp suppression-cache shows entries but hosts cannot communicate
Hosts on same subnet across VTEPs cannot reach each other via IP

failure_modes.description: VXLAN encapsulation fails because the underlay path between VTEPs traverses a sub-interface (e.g., router-on-a-stick or inter-VRF leaking via sub-interfaces). Many platforms do not support VXLAN encapsulation/decapsulation on sub-interfaces, causing silent packet drops.

resolutions.action: Redesign underlay to use only routed physical interfaces or SVIs
Redesign underlay to use only routed physical interfaces or SVIs
Redesign underlay to use only routed physical interfaces or SVIs
Avoid sub-interfaces in the VXLAN underlay path
Avoid sub-interfaces in the VXLAN underlay path
Avoid sub-interfaces in the VXLAN underlay path
Verify platform support for VXLAN on the transit interface type
Verify platform support for VXLAN on the transit interface type
Verify platform support for VXLAN on the transit interface type

root_causes.cause: Underlay path traverses a sub-interface that does not support VXLAN
Router-on-a-stick topology used in underlay between spine and leaf
Inter-VRF leaking via sub-interfaces in the VXLAN path

symptoms: VTEP peers shown as Up but no data traffic flows
Ping between VTEPs succeeds (underlay) but VXLAN-encapsulated traffic fails
Packet captures show VXLAN packets arriving at transit device but not forwarded

failure_modes.description: After a VM migration or host move, traffic to the host is black-holed because the old VTEP still has a stale local MAC entry and continues to attract traffic via its EVPN type-2 advertisement. The new VTEP has learned the MAC locally but the sequence number race has not resolved, or ARP suppression cache has a stale entry.

resolutions.action: Verify MAC mobility: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2' — check sequence numbers from both VTEPs
Verify MAC mobility: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2' — check sequence numbers from both VTEPs
Verify MAC mobility: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2' — check sequence numbers from both VTEPs
Clear stale ARP suppression entries: 'clear ip arp suppression-cache vlan <X>'
Clear stale ARP suppression entries: 'clear ip arp suppression-cache vlan <X>'
Clear stale ARP suppression entries: 'clear ip arp suppression-cache vlan <X>'
Check for stuck local MAC: 'show mac address-table address <mac>' on old VTEP
Check for stuck local MAC: 'show mac address-table address <mac>' on old VTEP
Check for stuck local MAC: 'show mac address-table address <mac>' on old VTEP
Tune duplicate detection thresholds if legitimate migrations trigger freezing
Tune duplicate detection thresholds if legitimate migrations trigger freezing
Tune duplicate detection thresholds if legitimate migrations trigger freezing

root_causes.cause: MAC mobility sequence number not incrementing fast enough
ARP suppression cache stale on intermediate VTEPs
Old VTEP not clearing local MAC entry promptly after port-down
Orphan port on old VTEP still forwarding for the migrated host

symptoms: Host unreachable after VM migration for 30-300 seconds
show l2route evpn mac all shows MAC on both old and new VTEPs
ARP suppression cache on remote VTEPs points to old VTEP IP
Gradual recovery as timers expire

messages.description: Encapsulated L2 frame with VXLAN header. The original Ethernet frame is encapsulated with a VXLAN header, UDP header, outer IP header, and outer Ethernet header.
messages.name: VXLAN Data Frame
messages.when_sent: When frame needs to cross the overlay (destination MAC is behind remote VTEP)

messages.description: Broadcast/Unknown-unicast/Multicast traffic handling. BUM traffic is replicated to all VTEPs in the VNI via either ingress replication (unicast to each peer) or multicast underlay group.
key_fields: Ingress Replication list or Multicast Group
messages.name: VXLAN BUM Frame
messages.when_sent: When destination is unknown or broadcast/multicast

purpose: Virtual Extensible LAN -- Layer 2 overlay over Layer 3 underlay using UDP encapsulation

states.description: VTEP (NVE) interface is administratively or operationally down. No VXLAN encapsulation or decapsulation is occurring.
states.name: DOWN

states.description: VTEP is operationally up with VNI mappings active. VXLAN encapsulation and decapsulation are functioning. Remote VTEP peers are reachable and traffic can traverse the overlay.
states.name: UP

states.description: Remote VTEP peer is unreachable. The local NVE interface may be up but one or more remote VTEPs cannot be contacted, typically due to an underlay routing failure or remote VTEP going offline.
states.name: PEER_DOWN

states.description: A specific VNI is inactive. The NVE interface may be up but an individual VLAN-to-VNI mapping is not operational, preventing traffic for that segment from crossing the overlay.
states.name: VNI_DOWN

timers.description: Time to retain remote host MAC/IP learned via data-plane in flood-and-learn mode
timers.name: host-reachability-hold
timers.tuning_advice: In flood-and-learn deployments, lower values increase convergence speed but also increase BUM flooding. In BGP EVPN mode this timer is superseded by EVPN MAC aging.

timers.description: Timeout for cached ARP entries in the VXLAN ARP suppression table
timers.name: ARP suppression cache timeout
timers.on_expiry: Entry removed, next ARP is flooded
timers.tuning_advice: Keep aligned with host ARP cache timeout. Too low causes excessive ARP flooding; too high risks stale entries.

transitions.trigger: NVE interface configured and source interface is up with routable IP
NVE source interface down or NVE shutdown
Remote VTEP becomes unreachable (underlay routing failure)
VLAN-to-VNI mapping resolved and NVE is up

notes.note: VXLAN supported on Catalyst 9000 with 'interface nve1'. Supports BGP EVPN control plane. Different CLI syntax from NX-OS -- uses 'member vni' under NVE interface.
VXLAN supported via BGP EVPN only (no flood-and-learn). Configured under 'l2vpn bridge group' and 'evpn'. NVE interface not used -- VXLAN encapsulation configured under bridge-domain.

VXLAN requires 'feature nv overlay' and 'feature vn-segment-vlan-based'. NVE interface configured as 'interface nve1'. Supports both flood-and-learn and BGP EVPN control plane. 'host-reachability protocol bgp' for EVPN mode.

CRITICAL: L3VNI SVI requires 'ip forward' for inter-subnet routing to work. Without it, the L3VNI shows Up in 'show nve vni' but routed traffic is silently dropped. Also requires 'no shutdown' and VRF membership on the SVI.

ARP suppression ('suppress-arp' under member VNI) requires anycast gateway to be configured on the SVI for proxy-ARP to work. Without 'fabric forwarding mode anycast-gateway' on the SVI, ARP requests are suppressed but no proxy response is generated, causing ARP failures.

In vPC VXLAN deployments, VLAN-to-VNI mapping must be identical on both peers. Mismatches cause NVE shutdown on the secondary peer via VXLAN consistency check. Verify with 'show vpc consistency-parameters global'.

vPC delay-restore timers are critical for VXLAN fabrics. After peer reload: 'delay restore' (default 150s) controls when vPC member ports come up, 'delay restore interface-vlan' (default 10s) controls DAG SVI restoration, and NVE 'source-interface hold-down-timer' (default 180s) controls when the VTEP starts forwarding. The NVE hold-down must be longer than delay restore to prevent post-reload black-holes.

VXLAN does not support sub-interfaces in the underlay path. If the underlay route to a remote VTEP traverses a sub-interface (e.g., router-on-a-stick), VXLAN encapsulated packets will be silently dropped. Use only routed physical interfaces, port-channels, or SVIs in the underlay.
